title: नमस्ते! Greetings & Introductions
subtitle: Hello, how are you, what's your name
grammar: Hindi uses the Devanagari script. The verb होना (honā - to be) conjugates: मैं हूँ (I am), आप हैं (you are formal), वह है (he/she is). Question words: क्या (what), कैसे (how), कहाँ (where).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: मैं ठीक हूँ, धन्यवाद। आप सुनाइए?
dialogues.transliteration: Maiṅ ṭhīk hūṅ, dhanyavād. Āp sunāie?

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: मैं भी ठीक हूँ। आपका नाम क्या है?
dialogues.transliteration: Maiṅ bhī ṭhīk hūṅ. Āpkā nām kyā hai?
dialogues.english: I'm also fine. What's your name?

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: मेरा नाम सारा है। आपसे मिलकर खुशी हुई!
dialogues.transliteration: Merā nām sārā hai. Āpse milkar khushī huī!
dialogues.english: My name is Sara. Pleased to meet you!

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'Hello, how are you?' in Hindi?
How do you say 'My name is...' in Hindi?
आपका _____ क्या है? (name)
Introduce yourself in Hindi: name, how you are, and say nice to meet you.
Someone says 'नमस्ते! आप कैसे हैं?' How do you reply?

title: अ आ क ख Devanagari & Nouns
subtitle: Reading Hindi script and basic noun genders
grammar: Hindi has 11 vowels and 33 consonants. Nouns have gender (masculine or feminine). Masculine often ends in -आ (लड़का), feminine in -ी (लड़की). The postposition का/के/की shows possession: राहुल का (Rahul's masc), सीता का (Sita's fem).

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'What is this?' in Hindi?
यह _____ किताब है (Rahul's)
Point to things around you and say 'यह ___ है' in Hindi.
Someone asks 'यह क्या है?' Point to a book and answer.

title: मैं सीखता हूँ Present Tense
subtitle: Daily routines and present actions
grammar: Present tense: verb stem + ता/ती/ते + हूँ/है/हैं/हो. Masculine singular: मैं बोलता हूँ (I speak), वह बोलता है (he speaks). Feminine singular: मैं बोलती हूँ (I speak f), वह बोलती है (she speaks). Plural: हम बोलते हैं (we speak), वे बोलते हैं (they speak).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: मैं एक कंपनी में काम करता हूँ।
dialogues.transliteration: Maiṅ ek kampanī meṅ kām kartā hūṅ.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, मैं रोज़ हिंदी पढ़ता हूँ।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, maiṅ roz hindī paṛhtā hūṅ.
dialogues.english: Yes, I study Hindi every day.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत अच्छा! तुम कितना समझते हो?
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut acchā! Tum kitnā samajhte ho?
dialogues.english: Very good! How much do you understand?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I learn Hindi every day'?
How do you say 'She speaks Hindi'?
वे रोज़ काम पर _____ हैं (go)
Tell the AI what you do every day. Use present tense.
A friend asks 'तुम क्या करते हो?' What do you answer?

title: एक-दो-तीन Numbers & Shopping
subtitle: Counting, prices, and bargaining
grammar: Numbers 1-100. Question words: कितना/कितने (how much/many). The postposition का/के/की marks price: पचास रुपए का (for 50 rupees). लेना (to take) and देना (to give) are key shopping verbs.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत महँगा है! तीस रुपए दीजिए?
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut mahaṅgā hai! Tīs rupe dījie?
dialogues.english: Too expensive! Give it for thirty?

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: ठीक है, मैं लेता हूँ। धन्यवाद!
dialogues.transliteration: Ṭhīk hai, maiṅ letā hūṅ. Dhanyavād!

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'How much is this?' in Hindi?
How do you say 'It is too expensive!' in Hindi?
यह दो _____ का है (hundred)
Practice: ask the price, say it's expensive, bargain to half price.
A shopkeeper says 'यह दो सौ रुपए का है.' How do you say 'too expensive'?

title: मेरा परिवार Family & Possessives
subtitle: Family members and possessive pronouns
grammar: Possessive pronouns: मेरा/मेरी (my), तेरा/तेरी (your intimate), आपका/आपकी (your formal), हमारा/हमारी (our), उसका/उसकी (his/her). The possessive agrees with the noun's gender: मेरा भाई (my brother), मेरी बहन (my sister).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: मेरे परिवार में माँ, पिता और एक छोटी बहन है।
dialogues.transliteration: Mere parivār meṅ māṅ, pitā aur ek choṭī bahan hai.
dialogues.english: In my family there's mother, father, and a younger sister.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: वह एक स्कूल में पढ़ाते हैं।
dialogues.transliteration: Vah ek skūl meṅ paṛhāte haiṅ.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत अच्छा! आपकी माँ भी पढ़ाती हैं?
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut acchā! Āpkī māṅ bhī paṛhātī haiṅ?
dialogues.english: Great! Does your mother also teach?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'my brother' in Hindi?
How do you say 'Who is in your family?' in Hindi?
यह मेरी _____ है (sister)
Describe your family in Hindi using possessive pronouns.
Someone asks 'आपके कितने भाई-बहन हैं?' Tell them how many siblings you have.

title: कल जाऊँगा Future Tense & Plans
subtitle: Talking about future plans and intentions
grammar: Future tense: stem + -ऊँगा/-एगा/-एँगे (masc), -ऊँगी/-एगी/-एँगी (fem). मैं जाऊँगा (I will go m), वह आएगी (she will come), हम देखेंगे (we will see). Time words: कल (tomorrow/yesterday), आज (today), परसों (day after), अगले हफ़्ते (next week).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगा। एक दोस्त से मिलूँगा।
dialogues.transliteration: Maiṅ dillī jāūṅgā. Ek dost se milūṅgā.
dialogues.english: I'll go to Delhi. I'll meet a friend.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.english: Oh! What time will you come back?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: शायद तीन बजे। फिर हम फिल्म देखेंगे?
dialogues.transliteration: Śāyad tīn baje. Phir ham film dekhẽge?
dialogues.english: Maybe at 3. Then we'll watch a movie?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I will go to Delhi tomorrow'?
How do you say 'We will watch a movie'?
वह कल _____ (will come)
Tell the AI what you will do tomorrow. Use future tense.
A friend asks 'कल क्या करोगे?' What will you tell them?

title: क्या हुआ? Past Tense & Stories
subtitle: Talking about past events and experiences
grammar: Simple past: verb stem + -आ/-ए/-ई. For transitive verbs, the subject takes ने (ergative): मैंने खाना खाया (I ate food). Intransitive verbs don't take ने: मैं बाज़ार गया (I went to market). Verb agrees with object in transitive sentences.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.english: I went to the market. Bought vegetables.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: एक दोस्त से मिला। हमने चाय पी।
dialogues.transliteration: Ek dost से milā. Hamne cāy pī.
dialogues.english: Met a friend. We drank tea.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत अच्छा! क्या तुमने खाना खाया?
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut acchā! Kyā tumne khānā khāyā?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, हमने साथ में खाना खाया।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, hamne sāth meṅ khānā khāyā.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'What did you do yesterday?' in Hindi?
How do you say 'I went to the market' in Hindi?
उसने चाय _____ (drank)
Tell the AI what you did yesterday. Use past tense with ने where needed.
A friend asks 'कल तुमने क्या किया?' Tell them in Hindi.

title: क्या खाएँगे? Food & Restaurants
subtitle: Ordering food, dietary needs, and restaurant talk
grammar: चाहिए (need/want) is impersonal: मुझे पानी चाहिए (I need water). चाहना (to want) is personal: मैं खाना चाहता हूँ (I want to eat). The future can be used for ordering: मैं दाल लूँगा (I'll take dal).

dialogues.speaker: W
dialogues.english: Here you go. What will you have?

dialogues.speaker: C
dialogues.hindi: मैं शाकाहारी हूँ। दाल और रोटी लूँगा।
dialogues.transliteration: Maiṅ śākāhārī hūṅ. Dāl aur roṭī lūṅgā.
dialogues.english: I'm vegetarian. I'll have dal and roti.

dialogues.speaker: C
dialogues.english: Yes, please give a bottle of water.

dialogues.speaker: W
dialogues.english: Will you have something sweet too?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I need water' in Hindi?
How do you say 'I am vegetarian' in Hindi?
मुझे एक गिलास _____ चाहिए (water)
Order at a restaurant: ask for menu, say you're vegetarian, order dal + roti + water.
A waiter asks 'आप क्या लेंगे?' Tell them what you want.

title: कहाँ है? Directions & Location
subtitle: Asking and giving directions, postpositions
grammar: Hindi uses postpositions after nouns: में (in), पर (on), के पास (near), के सामने (in front of), के बगल में (next to). Key question: कहाँ (where). Location responses use यहाँ (here), वहाँ (there).

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.english: Excuse me, where is the station?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: पाँच मिनट की दूरी है। बाएँ तरफ़ एक बड़ी इमारत है।
dialogues.transliteration: Pāṅc minaṭ kī dūrī hai. Bāeṅ taraph ek baṛī imārat hai.
dialogues.english: It's five minutes away. There's a big building on the left.

exercises.prompt: How do you ask 'Where is the station?' in Hindi?
How do you say 'Go straight, then turn right'?
किताब मेज़ _____ है (on)
Practice asking directions to a temple or station in Hindi.
Someone asks 'बाज़ार कहाँ है?' Give directions in Hindi.

title: बैठिए Commands & Requests
subtitle: Politely asking and telling people what to do
grammar: Imperatives have three levels of politeness. Formal (आप): stem + -इए — बैठिए, कीजिए, जाइए. Familiar (तुम): stem + -ओ — बैठो, करो, जाओ. Intimate (तू): just the stem — बैठ, कर, जा. Negatives use मत: मत जाइए (don't go).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: धन्यवाद। मुझे एक गिलास पानी दीजिए।
dialogues.transliteration: Dhanyavād. Mujhe ek gilās pānī dījie.
dialogues.english: Thanks. Please give me a glass of water.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.english: Here you go. Listen to this.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: माफ़ कीजिए, मैं बाहर जाता हूँ।
dialogues.transliteration: Māf kījie, maiṅ bāhar jātā hūṅ.

exercises.prompt: How do you politely say 'Please sit here'?
How do you say 'Please listen' in Hindi?
यहाँ मत _____ (don't go)
Politely ask someone to sit, give you water, and listen to you.
Someone says 'कृपया बैठिए'. How do you respond?

title: मुझे लगता है Opinions & Feelings
subtitle: Expressing likes, dislikes, and thoughts
grammar: पसंद होना (to like): मुझे यह पसंद है (I like this). अच्छा/बुरा लगना (to feel good/bad): मुझे यह अच्छा लगता है (I like this - lit: this feels good to me). मुझे लगता है (I think / it seems to me).

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: मुझे हिंदी बहुत पसंद है। थोड़ी मुश्किल है, लेकिन मज़ेदार है।
dialogues.transliteration: Mujhe hindī bahut pasand hai. Thoṛī muśkil hai, lekin mazedār hai.
dialogues.english: I like Hindi a lot. It's a bit difficult but fun.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: मुझे लगता है तुम जल्दी सीख जाओगे।
dialogues.transliteration: Mujhe lagtā hai tum jaldī sīkh jāoge.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: तुम्हें क्या लगता है, क्या मैं अच्छा बोलता हूँ?
dialogues.transliteration: Tumhe kyā lagtā hai, kyā maiṅ acchā boltā hūṅ?
dialogues.english: What do you think, do I speak well?

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बिल्कुल! तुम बहुत अच्छा बोलते हो।
dialogues.transliteration: Bilkul! Tum bahut acchā bolte ho.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I like Hindi' in Hindi?
How do you say 'What do you think?' in Hindi?
मुझे यह बहुत _____ लगता है (good)
Tell the AI your opinion about Hindi. Use पसंद है and लगता है.
Someone asks 'हिंदी कैसी लगती है?' Share your opinion.

title: मैंने किया है Perfective & Experiences
subtitle: Have you ever...? Talking about life experiences
grammar: Present perfect: verb stem + -आ/-ए/-ई + है/हैं/हूँ. मैंने खाया है (I have eaten). Intransitive: मैं गया हूँ (I have gone). कभी (ever) is used in questions: क्या तुम कभी भारत गए हो? कभी नहीं (never) for negatives.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: क्या तुम कभी भारत गए हो?
dialogues.transliteration: Kyā tum kabhī bhārat gae ho?
dialogues.english: Have you ever been to India?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, दो बार गया हूँ। बहुत सुंदर देश है।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, do bār gayā hūṅ. Bahut sundar deś hai.
dialogues.english: Yes, I've been twice. It's a very beautiful country.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.english: Have you seen the Taj Mahal?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, देखा है। बहुत सुंदर इमारत है!
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, dekhā hai. Bahut sundar imārat hai!
dialogues.english: Yes, I've seen it. A beautiful building!

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: मैंने कभी नहीं देखा। मैं जाना चाहता हूँ।
dialogues.transliteration: Maine kabhī nahīṅ dekhā. Maiṅ jānā cāhtā hūṅ.
dialogues.english: I've never seen it. I want to go.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: ज़रूर जाओ। तुम्हें बहुत अच्छा लगेगा!
dialogues.transliteration: Zarūr jāo. Tumhe bahut acchā lagegā!
dialogues.english: Definitely go. You'll really like it!

exercises.prompt: How do you ask 'Have you been to India?'
exercises.answer: क्या तुम कभी भारत गए हो?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I have never seen it'?
तुमने कभी दाल _____ है? (have eaten)
Tell the AI about a place you've visited. Use perfective tense.
Someone asks 'क्या आपने कभी भारतीय खाना खाया है?' Answer them.

title: कर सकते हैं Compound Verbs & Ability
subtitle: Can, could, and compound verbs for complete actions
grammar: सकना (can) follows the stem: मैं कर सकता हूँ (I can do). Compound verbs add nuance: कर लेना (do for oneself), कर देना (do completely), खा लेना (eat up), आ जाना (arrive - lit: come + go). लेना (take) and देना (give) as auxiliaries change who the action benefits.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: क्या तुम हिंदी बोल सकते हो?
dialogues.transliteration: Kyā tum hindī bol sakte ho?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.english: Yes, I can speak a little.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: क्या तुमने अपना काम कर लिया?
dialogues.transliteration: Kyā tumne apnā kām kar liyā?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, मैंने कर दिया। आराम कर सकते हो।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, maine kar diyā. Ārām kar sakte ho.
dialogues.english: Yes, I did it all. You can rest.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत अच्छा! तुम बहुत थक गए हो?
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut acchā! Tum bahut thak gae ho?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: थोड़ा थक गया हूँ, लेकिन खुश हूँ।
dialogues.transliteration: Thoṛā thak gayā hūṅ, lekin khush hūṅ.
dialogues.english: I'm a bit tired but happy.

exercises.prompt: How do you ask 'Can you speak Hindi?'
exercises.answer: क्या तुम हिंदी बोल सकते हो?

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'I finished my work' (for myself)?
वह बहुत _____ गया है (has gotten tired)
Tell the AI what you can do in Hindi. Use सकता/सकती हूँ.
Someone asks 'क्या आप यह काम कर सकते हैं?' Answer them.

title: इससे अच्छा Comparisons & Relatives
subtitle: Comparing things and using relative clauses
grammar: Comparisons: X + से + adjective: यह उससे अच्छा है (This is better than that). Superlative: सबसे + adjective: यह सबसे अच्छा है (This is the best). Relative-correlative: जो (who/which) and वह (that): जो आदमी वहाँ है, वह मेरा दोस्त है (The man who is there is my friend).

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.english: This book is better than that one.

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, लेकिन वह सबसे अच्छी है।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, lekin vah sabse acchī hai.
dialogues.english: Yes, but that one is the best.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: जो आदमी वहाँ खड़ा है, वह कौन है?
dialogues.transliteration: Jo ādmī vahāṅ khaṛā hai, vah kaun hai?
dialogues.english: Who is the man standing there?

dialogues.speaker: B
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, वह मुझसे ज़्यादा लंबे हैं।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, vah mujhse zyādā lambe haiṅ.
dialogues.english: Yes, he is taller than me.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'This is better than that' in Hindi?
How do you say 'This is the best' in Hindi?
यह उससे बड़ा _____ (is)
Compare two things in Hindi using से and सबसे.
Someone says 'यह किताब अच्छी है, लेकिन वह सबसे अच्छी है.' Do you agree?

title: एक कहानी Narratives & Storytelling
subtitle: Telling stories, connecting ideas, describing events
grammar: Connectors for narratives: फिर (then), तब (then), उसके बाद (after that), इसलिए (therefore), क्योंकि (because), अचानक (suddenly), अंत में (finally). Use all three tenses together to tell a complete story.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: बहुत दिन पहले की बात है, मैं दिल्ली गया था।
dialogues.transliteration: Bahut din pahle kī bāt hai, maiṅ dillī gayā thā.
dialogues.english: A long time ago, I went to Delhi.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: अचानक मेरी तबीयत खराब हो गई।
dialogues.transliteration: Acānak merī tabīyat kharāb ho gaī.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: एक अच्छे डॉक्टर से मिला। उसने मुझे दवा दी।
dialogues.transliteration: Ek acche ḍokṭar se milā. Usne mujhe davā dī.
dialogues.english: I met a good doctor. He gave me medicine.

dialogues.speaker: A
dialogues.hindi: हाँ, दो दिन में मैं ठीक हो गया।
dialogues.transliteration: Hāṅ, do din meṅ maiṅ ṭhīk ho gayā.
dialogues.english: Yes, in two days I got better.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'A long time ago' in Hindi?
How do you say 'Then what happened?' in Hindi?
मैं दिल्ली _____ था (had gone)
Tell the AI a short story about something that happened to you. Use connectors.
A friend says 'बहुत दिन पहले मैं भारत गया था...' Ask them what happened.
